creator: Trond.Endrestol@ximalas.info
text:
(In reply to Gerald Pfeifer from comment #3)

The problem has vanished with lang/gcc aka gcc-4.8.5_2:

trond@enterprise:~/java/hello-world>cat Hello.java
public class Hello {
  public static void Main(String[] Args) {
    System.out.println("hello world");
  }
};
trond@enterprise:~/java/hello-world>gcj48 -v -c Hello.java
Using built-in specs.
Reading specs from /usr/local/lib/gcc48/gcc/x86_64-portbld-freebsd10.3/4.8.5/../../../libgcj.spec
rename spec startfile to startfileorig
rename spec lib to liborig

COLLECT_GCC_OPTIONS='-fsaw-java-file' '-v' '-c' '-fbootclasspath=./:/usr/local/share/java/libgcj-4.8.5.jar' '-g1' '-specs=libgcj.spec' '-shared-libgcc' '-mtune=generic' '-march=x86-64'
